title: Intensely Roasted Saudi Arabian Beef Tomatoes Flatbread
description: A dense and flavorful Saudi Arabian dish featuring roasted beef tomatoes on a laminated puff flatbread, perfect for a hangover cure, and pescatarian friendly by substituting beef with portobello mushrooms or eggplant for a similar texture.

ingredients.name: portobello mushrooms
ingredients.notes: or eggplant for a similar texture

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut the pastry into 4 equal squares, about 6 inches per side.
In a large bowl, toss the beef tomatoes with olive oil, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and zaatar until they are evenly coated.
Arrange the tomatoes on one half of each pastry square, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Top the tomatoes with portobello mushrooms or eggplant slices.
Fold the other half of the pastry square over the filling to form a triangle or a square shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the pastry with a little bit of olive oil.
Place the flatbreads on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
